title: rosa agrestis f. parvifolia
intro: Rosa agrestis f. parvifolia, also known as wild rose or rose hips, is a plant species in the family Rosaceae. It is a shrub with fragrant flowers that are used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing stress and anxiety, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, as well as providing vitamins and minerals such as vitamin C and iron. Rosa agrestis f. parvifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as used in jams, jellies, and other culinary applications.
